name: phishing_triage
description: a phishing-report triage world; actions report, analyze, quarantine, educate

card.description: a phishing-report triage world; actions report, analyze, quarantine, educate

rules: 'report' adds one email to the pending-analysis queue and raises risk by 5 (capped at 100).
'analyze' moves one email from pending to confirmed (no-op when pending is 0).
'quarantine' moves one email from confirmed to quarantined and lowers risk by 10, floored at 0 (no-op when confirmed is 0).
'educate' trains one user (educated += 1) and lowers risk by 3, floored at 0.
risk is clamped to the range [0, 100]; counts never go negative.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    name = action["name"]
    if name == "report":
        # A user flags a suspicious email: it joins the analysis queue and raises
        # exposure until someone handles it.
        s["pending"] = s["pending"] + 1
        s["risk"] = min(100, s["risk"] + 5)
    elif name == "analyze":
        # An analyst triages the oldest pending report and confirms it as phishing.
        if s["pending"] > 0:
            s["pending"] = s["pending"] - 1
            s["confirmed"] = s["confirmed"] + 1
    elif name == "quarantine":
        # Pull a confirmed phishing message from inboxes: the threat is removed
        # and org risk drops materially.
        if s["confirmed"] > 0:
            s["confirmed"] = s["confirmed"] - 1
            s["quarantined"] = s["quarantined"] + 1
            s["risk"] = max(0, s["risk"] - 10)
    elif name == "educate":
        # Train users so fewer take the bait next time: a smaller, steady risk cut.
        s["educated"] = s["educated"] + 1
        s["risk"] = max(0, s["risk"] - 3)
    return s

emit.report: triage: {pending} pending, {confirmed} confirmed, {quarantined} quarantined, {educated} trained; risk {risk}
